reason: Attempt 1 correctly identified that the original ownership plan is not feasible as written: `MLF.Binding.Tree` already depends on `MLF.Binding.Validation`, so making validation import tree-owned helpers creates a hard module cycle. No code changed, so the duplicate helper cluster still exists and the plan’s acceptance criteria were not met. The next attempt should keep the verifier-approved goal of removing the duplicate helper cluster and preserving thesis-exact behavior, but change the ownership shape to a cycle-free shared helper module (or equivalent minimal extraction) that both `Tree` and `Validation` can import.

PLAN_DELTA.what_failed: The original plan assumed `MLF.Binding.Tree` could remain the sole owner while `MLF.Binding.Validation` consumed those helpers, but the existing `Tree -> Validation` dependency makes that ownership direction impossible without introducing an import cycle or broader reorganization.
what_changes_now: Replace the 'Tree is sole owner' step with extraction of `collectBoundChildren`, `tyVarChildFilter`, and `boundFlexChildren` into a new minimal shared binding-helper module under `src/MLF/Binding/` that has no dependency on either `Tree` or `Validation`.

Update both `src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs` and `src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs` to import that shared helper module, deleting both in-module duplicate implementations so there is still exactly one live implementation of the helper cluster.

Keep the regression guard in `test/BindingSpec.hs`, but phrase it around the binder-selection / bound-flex-child invariant rather than module ownership, so the test protects thesis-defined behavior independent of where the helper lives.
Update `mlf2.cabal` if needed to include the new module, and add the `CHANGELOG.md` maintenance note describing helper-cluster extraction and deduplication.
Run targeted binding-spec coverage first, then the full gate `cabal build all && cabal test`.
PLAN_DELTA.why_next_attempt_should_work: A leaf shared helper module removes the `Tree <-> Validation` cycle while still achieving the accepted semantic goal: one implementation of the bound-child query helpers, reused by both call sites, with no intended change to Q(n) behavior.
